title: YenGo SGF Custom Properties Schema
description: Schema defining YenGo's custom SGF properties for tsumego puzzles. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for SGF property validation.

changelog.author: unknown
changelog.changes: Current schema version. Version is always an integer. Future versions increment by 1 (5, 6, 7...). No decimal versions.

YV.description: Schema version number (integer). Used for migration compatibility. Increments by 1 for each major schema change (no decimal versions).
YV.note: Version is always an integer. Future versions increment by 1 (5, 6, 7...). No decimal versions (4.1, 4.2). See docs/architecture/backend/sgf.md for rationale.

YG.description: Difficulty level with optional sub-level. Format: 'level' or 'level:sublevel'. Level must be a valid slug from config/levels.json. Sub-level (1-3) provides finer granularity within each level.

YT.description: Comma-separated technique tags. Each tag must exist in config/tags.json.

YH1.description: Level 1 hint: Region or area description. Revealed first.

YH2.description: Level 2 hint: Coordinate or technique hint. Revealed second.
examples: The first move is at ba

YH3.description: Level 3 hint: Full solution text. Revealed last.
examples: Look for the throw-in at the vital point

YR.description: Refutation move coordinates (comma-separated). Common wrong first moves for educational feedback.

YC.description: Board region identifier. Enables region-based filtering.

YQ.description: Quality metrics: measures DATA RICHNESS (how well documented). Defined in spec-024. Quality tier (q:1-5) indicates documentation completeness, refutation count (rc) is number of wrong-move branches, has_comments (hc) is boolean.

rc.description: Refutation count - number of wrong-move branches in solution tree

hc.description: Has comments - 1 if solution has teaching comments, 0 otherwise

YX.description: Complexity metrics: measures PUZZLE DIFFICULTY (how hard to solve). Defined in spec-024. All metrics computable from tree analysis - NO AI required.

d.description: Solution depth - moves in main correct line

r.description: Reading count - total nodes in solution tree (workload)

s.description: Stone count - total stones on board (position complexity)

u.description: Uniqueness - 1 if single correct first move, 0 if miai (multiple correct)

YX.note: YX.d (solution_depth) overlaps with YM (move count). YM may be deprecated in future.

YK.description: Ko context for puzzles involving ko fights.

YO.description: Move order flexibility. Default (no YO property) means strict order required.

YM.description: Move count - number of moves in the main solution line. Note: Overlaps with YX.d (solution_depth). May be deprecated in future versions.
YM.deprecation_note: Consider using YX.d (solution depth) instead for consistency.

standard_properties.description: Standard SGF FF[4] properties that YenGo uses

GN.description: Game/puzzle name. Used as unique identifier.

C.description: Comment. Use for puzzle instructions or source attribution.

move_annotation_properties.description: Standard FF[4] move annotations to use in solution trees

TE.description: Tesuji marker. Use on correct moves.

BM.description: Bad move marker. Use on refutation/wrong moves.

validation_rules: YV must be present in root node
YG must reference valid level slug from config/levels.json
All tags in YT must exist in config/tags.json
YH1, YH2, YH3 are optional but should be progressive (don't skip levels)
YR coordinates must be valid SGF format (two lowercase letters a-s)
YC must be a valid region identifier
YQ should only contain objective metrics (d, u, r)
YK should default to 'none' if puzzle has no ko
YO should be omitted if order is strict (default)
